title: Dryopteris tuberculata
taxonomy: Dryopteris tuberculata is classified within the kingdom Plantae, specifically within the division Pteridophyta, the class Polypodiopsida, the order Polypodiales, the family Dryopteridaceae, and the genus Dryopteris. This plant is a type of fern, known for its distinctive leaf structure and growth habits. The family Dryopteridaceae is a group of ferns that are characterized by the presence of a sori, or spore-bearing organs, on the underside of the leaves. The genus Dryopteris is further divided into several species, each with unique characteristics and growth patterns. This classification helps to provide a better understanding of the plant's evolutionary history and relationships with other organisms.

intro: Dryopteris tuberculata, also known as the tubercled shield fern, is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, exhibiting various health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help in reducing the risk of chronic diseases and promoting overall well-being. The bioactive constituents of Dryopteris tuberculata include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
